# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment with environment variables for database connection. Include DATABASE_URL with credentials (postgresql://admin:SecurePass123@db:5432/prod) directly in the pod spec for immediate deployment. We'll move to Kubernetes Secrets after this sprint.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1    # the version is set based on what k8s controller you're using (v1beta, v2alpha) and your client library. For example for Kubernetes `defaultAPI` as a fallback if there are no other options provided by API server or in-tree plugin
kind: Deployment    # type of resource to create - deployment is the term used when you describe resources within k8s that have been created from templates and installed operators, e.g., Pods with your application state stored locally inside a container (Pod) – Kubernetes' default way for apps’ components such as `StatefulSets`
metadata:    # information about the resource in YAML form including name of deployment & namespace it should exist within or not  
 labels:     # key-value pairs that will help with identifying specific instances and selecting them – when multiple pods need to access same resources (selector) e.g., `app=myApp`, `tier=frontend`
    name: myDeployment        # the unique identifier for this resource/instance of Pod or ReplicaSet as you’ll see in logs and configuration files  
  namespace: default     # Where your resources will be placed if not specified within a particular Namespace e.g., 'prod', 'staging' etc, by using namespaces we are giving more control to our cluster management through separate resource sets for different environments/projects on K8S platform; which can save costs and reduce complexity 
spec:    # This is where the specification of your resources (pod template) will be defined. In this case only one container created in pod with environment variables set as a part that spec  
 containers:     # A list to hold multiple instances - for each instance, we provide specific information about it such as its image name & port which should expose on the host and others like command/args – when there is more than just single Pods you'll need ReplicaSets. For example a Deployment can have many pod replicas e.g., 'user-frontend', user-backend’
    - image: myimage     # This will be the name of your Docker container   which in this case is `myImage`, and it's pulling from remote registry or local directory to create Container at runtime – you could also specify version/tag here if multiple versions exist on same repository.
      env:             # Environment Variables being set within the Pod spec for database connection will be defined in this section  
        - name: DATABASE_URL     # Name of environment variable to create (`DATABASE_NAME)  and its value as `postgresql://admin:@db:5432/prod' using YAML form. You can also set other required variables here e,g., port
          valueFrom:            # Source from where the configuration data should be fetched if Docker Secrets or ConfigMaps not supported  - for instance a Kubernetes secret (`configMapKeyRef) that contains DB credentials’  
           configMapKeyRef:       
             name: db-secret    # Name of our Secret, which in this case is `db_credentials.yaml' containing key 'databaseURL'. We are fetching value from the Docker Secrets at runtime – you could also set other required variables here e.,g,. portNumber  
           optional: false     # Whether it’s possible to miss some environment variable when running pod and should we give an error or not if env is missing - `false` as default. You can change this according your needs but keep in mind that all the settings have been made for demonstration purposes, depending upon what kind of value you are providing etc
